replication_study.hypothesis: Participants told they are being observed (Observed group) will show a higher proportion of deontological responses for personal moral dilemmas (operationalized as RH_PMD) than participants told responses are anonymous (Control group).
replication_study.study_type: Experimental (laboratory behavioral experiment; between-subjects manipulation: Condition = Observed vs Control; within-subjects: dilemma type).

data_plan.source_type: Behavioral experiment data file (SPSS .sav) containing moral-dilemma responses, confidence ratings, reaction times, lexical decision task measures, and questionnaires.
data_plan.wave_or_subset: Full provided dataset (no waves). If desired, analyses may be restricted to participants with complete main-task data; primary plan uses the full sample with listwise deletion for missing outcome variables.
data_plan.sample_size: 160 (rows in SCORE_all data.sav; see dataset shape) — significantly larger than the original N=48 (post_registration.json).
data_plan.unit_of_analysis: Individual participants (each row in the .sav corresponds to one participant).
data_plan.access_details: File included in the provided input bundle at data/original/39/input/replication_data/SCORE_all data.sav. For reproduction in other environments, place SCORE_all data.sav in /app/data/ (script attempts to locate file at /app/data and at the provided repository path).

qualification.explanation: This dataset is qualified for replication because it contains the same operationalized outcome variables and manipulation indicator as the original experiment (Condition, RH_PMD, RH_NMD, RH_CMD, D and U PD parameters, confidence and RTs, LDT RTs). The SPSS syntax (SCORE_syntax.sps) shows the same variable computations used in the original analyses (e.g., APP_* recoding and RH_PMD = APP_PMD/20), and the .sav file contains these computed variables (see column names in the loaded dataset). Thus the dataset preserves high fidelity to the original experimental measures while providing a new/larger sample for a robustness test.
qualification.similarity_to_original: Same experimental measures and computed indices: main-task item variables APP1_main..APP50_main and derived RH_PMD/RH_NMD/RH_CMD (see SCORE_syntax.sps for computations) and questionnaire composites (IRI_ALL, NFCog, Fear_ALL) match the original study's constructs described in post_registration.json ('main task: 50 dilemmas (20 personal, 15 impersonal, 15 non-moral)'; PD pretest for D/U). The original study description and hypotheses are documented in post_registration.json (Sections: data, method, results). The focal claim/hypothesis is documented in initial_details.txt and post_registration.json.
qualification.deviation_from_original: Key deviations: (1) Sample size and sample composition differ — the original study reports N=48 (24 OBS, 24 CON) (post_registration.json), while the provided dataset contains N=160 (data/original/39/input/replication_data/SCORE_all data.sav). This makes the replication an independent/extended-sample test rather than a direct reanalysis. (2) Any differences in participant recruitment, language, or experimental context are not fully documented in the provided metadata; post_registration.json noted stimuli were translated into Korean in the original study. These potential contextual differences mean the replication tests generalizability across a different/larger sample. The specific evidence for the original N=48 comes from post_registration.json and the claim/hypotheses are in initial_details.txt.
qualification.references: Original study design and sample: data/original/39/input/post_registration.json (fields: original_study.data, original_study.method, original_study.results); Focal claim/hypothesis: data/original/39/input/initial_details.txt; Variable definitions & computations: data/original/39/input/replication_data/SCORE_syntax.sps; Actual dataset and sample size: data/original/39/input/replication_data/SCORE_all data.sav (columns and shape).

data_plan.notes: The SPSS syntax file (SCORE_syntax.sps) shows the exact recoding and composite computations used by the original authors; the Python script implements these recodings. The dataset as provided already contains many derived variables (RH_PMD, CONF_PMD, RT_PMD, D, U). Potential caveats: (1) The dataset has many columns; some RT by-response-type variables (e.g., RT_PMD_Yes) were computed in Excel according to the syntax comments — those cell-level splits may not be present and the script uses available aggregated columns. (2) Variable value encodings and missing-data codes (system-missing vs numeric codes like -4) are handled where indicated in the syntax; however if there are undocumented codes the script may need adjustment. (3) For reproducibility the script writes outputs to /app/data/ (replication_summary.csv and replication_processed_data.csv).

steps: Place SCORE_all data.sav in /app/data/ or use the provided path; ensure replication_analysis__py.py is in the replication_data folder of the study.

Run replication_analysis__py.py (Python 3.8+) which: (a) loads SCORE_all data.sav via pyreadstat, (b) implements SPSS recodes and composite computations from SCORE_syntax.sps (D, U, RH_* composites, CONF_* recodes, RT means, questionnaires), (c) computes group descriptive statistics, (d) runs primary tests (two-sample comparisons and a mixed ANOVA for Condition x Dilemma type), and (e) saves outputs to /app/data/replication_summary.csv and processed data to /app/data/replication_processed_data.csv.

Primary test: compare RH_PMD between Condition groups (Observed vs Control) using an independent-samples t-test (Welch's t-test) and report means, t-statistic, p-value, and Cohen's d. Compute 2x3 mixed ANOVA (Condition x dilemma_type) on RH_PMD/RH_NMD/RH_CMD to test the interaction (replicating GLM in SPSS).

Secondary tests: group main effects on CONF_ALL and RT_ALL and within-dilemma-type comparisons; LDT RT comparisons by category (LDT_RT_Warm vs LDT_RT_Comp), and PD analysis (U and D) using the formulas in the SPSS syntax. Follow the SPSS post-hoc correction approach (Bonferroni) for multiple comparisons.
Document and save all outputs, including effect sizes (partial eta-squared for ANOVA; Cohen's d for t-tests), descriptive tables, and the processed dataset.
planned_method.models: Mixed-design ANOVA (between: Condition; within: Dilemma type with 3 levels — personal/impersonal/non-moral) implemented with pingouin.stats or statsmodels; independent-samples t-tests (Welch) for planned contrasts; PD parameter computations (D and U) via the formulas in the SPSS syntax; post-hoc tests with Bonferroni correction.
planned_method.outcome_variable: Primary: RH_PMD (proportion of deontological responses in personal moral dilemmas). Secondary: RH_NMD, RH_CMD, CONF_PMD/CONF_ALL, RT_PMD/RT_ALL, LDT_RT_Warm and LDT_RT_Comp, PD-parameters D and U.
planned_method.independent_variables: Between-subjects: Condition (Observed vs Control). Within-subjects (for repeated measures): dilemma_type (RH_PMD, RH_NMD, RH_CMD) and, for other analyses, LDT word category (Reputation, Competence, Warmth, Neutral).
planned_method.control_variables: Baseline PD deontological parameter D (from pretest) may be included as a covariate where appropriate (D, U exist in dataset per syntax). Other exploratory covariates: sex, age, NFCog, Fear_ALL, IRI_ALL (available in dataset).
planned_method.tools_software: Python 3.8+; key packages: pandas, numpy, scipy, statsmodels, pyreadstat, pingouin. The script replication_analysis__py.py is provided and saves outputs to /app/data/.

planned_estimation_and_test.estimation: Group means (proportion of deontological responses), differences in means, t-statistics, F-statistics for ANOVA, effect sizes (Cohen's d, partial eta-squared), PD parameters D and U.
planned_estimation_and_test.test: Independent-samples t-test (Welch) for RH_PMD between conditions; 2x3 mixed ANOVA for Condition x dilemma_type (F-test). Post-hoc pairwise tests with Bonferroni correction where applicable.

planned_method.missing_data_handling: Listwise deletion for analyses requiring particular dependent variables (e.g., drop participants with missing RH_PMD for RH_PMD analyses). For composite scores, compute means across available items per SPSS approach (mean of available items) unless missingness exceeds a threshold (document any cases with heavy missingness).
planned_method.multiple_testing_policy: Bonferroni correction for planned post-hoc pairwise comparisons following the ANOVA, consistent with the original analysis. For exploratory tests, report uncorrected p-values but flag them as exploratory.
planned_method.inference_criteria: Two-tailed tests, alpha = 0.05. For directional replication claims (original found OBS > CON for RH_PMD), report both p-values and effect sizes; declare replication support if the effect is in the same direction and statistically significant at alpha 0.05, and interpret effect-size similarity (e.g., Cohen's d magnitude). For multiple comparisons, apply Bonferroni-adjusted thresholds.

files.replication_analysis__py.py: Python script that (1) loads SCORE_all data.sav (pyreadstat), (2) implements SPSS recodes and composite calculations from SCORE_syntax.sps (recomputes NFCog, IRI subscales, Fear composites, recodes APP_main and CONF_main, computes APP_PMD/APP_NMD/APP_CMD and RH_* measures, RT means, D and U PD parameters), (3) computes descriptive statistics grouped by Condition, (4) executes primary statistical tests (independent t-test for RH_PMD; mixed ANOVA for Condition x dilemma_type), and (5) writes outputs to /app/data/replication_summary.csv and processed data to /app/data/replication_processed_data.csv. The script attempts to find the .sav in /app/data first but will fall back to the provided repository path.
files.SCORE_all data.sav: Primary dataset (SPSS .sav) with participant-level responses; used as input by replication_analysis__py.py (already present in the input directory).
files.SCORE_syntax.sps: Original SPSS syntax provided by authors. Used as the source to implement the exact recoding and composite computations in replication_analysis__py.py. The script mirrors recoding and composite computations from this file.
files.SCORE_codebook.xlsx: Codebook with variable descriptions (not executed by the script). Useful for interpretation and cross-checking variable labels.

codebase.notes: The Python script is the designated entrypoint for execution. It uses pyreadstat to read the .sav file and pingouin/statsmodels for statistical tests. All file I/O in the script saves outputs to /app/data/ to comply with repository rules. If you want to re-run analyses in a different environment, ensure the .sav file is placed at /app/data/SCORE_all data.sav or update the path in the script. The script preserves the SPSS logic; where SPSS comments note Excel post-processing (e.g., response-type-separated confidence or RT by response), the script uses the available aggregated variables (CONF_PMD_Yes etc. may have been computed externally in the provided dataset).

analysis.instructions: 1) Ensure SCORE_all data.sav is present in /app/data/ (or the script's fallback path). 2) From the repository root, run python data/original/39/input/replication_data/replication_analysis__py.py (or cd to its folder and run python replication_analysis__py.py). 3) The script will create /app/data/replication_summary.csv with descriptive statistics and primary test results and /app/data/replication_processed_data.csv with the processed dataset and computed composites. 4) Inspect replication_summary.csv for group means, t-statistics, ANOVA table (F, p, partial eta-squared), and effect sizes. 5) If needed, refine analyses (e.g., include D as covariate in ANCOVA) by editing replication_analysis__py.py and re-running.
analysis.comparison_metrics: Compare the original and replication results using: (a) direction of effect on RH_PMD (OBS > CON), (b) group mean difference and 95% CI for RH_PMD, (c) statistical significance (p-value), (d) effect size (Cohen's d for RH_PMD), and (e) ANOVA interaction F and partial eta-squared for Condition x dilemma_type. Also compare the RD (RH_PMD means) and RT and Confidence group differences and LDT_RT Warm vs Competence differences; report whether replication effect sizes are similar in magnitude to original (original reported Cohen's d=0.70 for RH_PMD and partial eta-squared=0.094 for the interaction per post_registration.json).
